methods.description: This method describes the conditions under which plant genotypes were grown and manipulated to study their development under controlled environmental conditions.

steps.description: The experiment was conducted in two periods. In the first period (March 2013), eight genotypes (CLN1621F, CL5915-93D4-1-0-3, CL1131-0-0-13-0-6, CLN475BC1F2-265-4-19, CLN65-349D5-2-0, CL5915-206D4-2-2-0-4, CL5915-153D4-3-3-0 and M-82) were used and in the second period (April 2013), nine additional genotypes were used (LA2854, LA1478, LA0417, LA1584, LA1580, LA1719, Nagcarlang, Saladette and SWEET4).
steps.searchable_summary: Select and categorize plant genotypes for two experimental periods

steps.description: Plants were grown in the greenhouse of Unifarm (Wageningen University & Research Centre, The Netherlands) at 25°C day and 19°C night under 12-18 h of natural day light for 1 month (control condition).
steps.searchable_summary: Grow plants in greenhouse under specified temperature and light conditions

steps.description: When the first flowers appeared, plants were moved and placed into a 15 m2 climate chamber using a randomized design.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer plants to climate chamber using randomized design

steps.description: The climate chamber was conditioned at 32/26°C, with 12 h of day light provided with fluorescent tubes at a photon flux density of 500 lmol and 60% humidity.
steps.searchable_summary: Set climate chamber conditions for temperature, light, and humidity

steps.description: All flowers and buds from each plant were removed with forceps to assure that new flowers have been completely developed under the climate chamber conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove existing flowers and buds from plants
outputs: plants with flowers and buds removed

steps.description: 2 weeks were needed to produce new open flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: Allow 2 weeks for new flower development
inputs: plants with removed flowers and buds
